ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Clear, text-based PDF with structured fields. All fields legible.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Clear, text-based PDF. All fields legible. Signed by Th.Brunius & Co AB.

lc_check.lc_validation_details: The invoice contains 'L/C No.: SM024IL005773900' in the body text. However, the PAYMENT TERMS field states 'Irrevocable LC At Sight', and the L/C No. field in the invoice body uses the explicit label 'L/C No.:' which is a separate field label followed by a number. This constitutes a valid LC reference per rule 2.2. HOWEVER, the LC reference appears in the INVOICE, not in the CERTIFICATE. Rule 2.2 states: 'Undantaget ska tillämpas när certifikatet innehåller en uttrycklig hänvisning till ett specifikt och individuellt LC-nummer.' The certificate (Document A) does NOT contain any LC reference. Therefore, the LC exception is NOT activated.

Invoice signature block: 'Th.Brunius & Co AB' at bottom of page 1

criteria_checked.details: Certificate does not contain an explicit invoice number reference.
Certificate consignor 'Th.Brunius & Co AB' is identifiable in the invoice header as 'Th. Brunius & Co AB' (same entity after normalization of spacing).
Certificate consignee 'Xiamen Port Commerce Information Co., Ltd.' is not found anywhere in the invoice. Invoice shows 'Hongkong Topway Trading Co., Limited' as both SOLD TO and CONSIGNED TO.

pairing_validation.details: At least one criterion (consignor) is met. Pairing validation passed.

checkpoint_results.certificate_value: Th.Brunius & Co AB, PO Box 2516, SE-403 17, Gothenburg, Sweden
checkpoint_results.invoice_value: Th. Brunius & Co AB, VAT NO: SE556011055201, PO Box 2516, SE-403 17 Gothenburg, Sweden

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name in all invoice files and pages.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate consignor 'Th.Brunius & Co AB' is directly identifiable in the invoice as the issuer: 'Th. Brunius & Co AB' in the header/address block and signature block. The only difference is a space after 'Th.' which is a minor formatting variation (normalization of extra spaces). The company name is the same juridical entity.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur - consignor name found directly in invoice issuer block and signature.
Verification of company name and country.
Country verified via explicit address block (Sweden) and VAT prefix SE.

normalizations_applied: Space normalization: 'Th.Brunius' vs 'Th. Brunius' (extra space after dot)
checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor: 'Th.Brunius & Co AB, PO Box 2516, SE-403 17, Gothenburg, Sweden'. Invoice issuer: 'Th. Brunius & Co AB, PO Box 2516, SE-403 17 Gothenburg, Sweden'. Company name differs only by a space after 'Th.' — this is a minor formatting difference per normalization rules (extra spaces). Address: PO Box 2516 matches. Postcode SE-403 17 matches. City Gothenburg matches. Country Sweden matches explicitly in address and via VAT prefix SE. Conclusion: MATCH.

checkpoint_results.certificate_value: Xiamen Port Commerce Information Co., Ltd., 6/F, No. 31 North Donggang Road, Xiamen Area of China (Fujian) Pilot Free Trade Zone, China
checkpoint_results.invoice_value: SOLD TO: Hongkong Topway Trading Co., Limited, Hong Kong; CONSIGNED TO: Hongkong Topway Trading Co., Limited, Hong Kong

comparison_steps.description: Check SOLD TO and CONSIGNED TO fields (primary billing fields) for certificate consignee.
comparison_steps.details: Invoice SOLD TO: 'Hongkong Topway Trading Co., Limited' in Hong Kong. Invoice CONSIGNED TO: 'Hongkong Topway Trading Co., Limited' in Hong Kong. Certificate consignee: 'Xiamen Port Commerce Information Co., Ltd.' in China. The company names are entirely different entities — 'Xiamen Port Commerce Information' vs 'Hongkong Topway Trading'. These are not the same juridical party.

comparison_steps.step: 4.2.0.1 - Check alternative fields (Delivery address, Receiver, Dealer, etc.)
comparison_steps.description: Search for 'Xiamen Port Commerce Information' in any other field in the invoice.
comparison_steps.details: The name 'Xiamen Port Commerce Information' does not appear anywhere in the invoice text. The invoice contains no delivery address, receiver, dealer, or importer field that mentions this company.

comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate consignee can be found in Ship-To/Delivery address.
comparison_steps.details: No Ship-To or Delivery Address field exists in the invoice. The certificate consignee cannot be found anywhere in the invoice.

comparison_steps.step: 4.1.3.1 - Juridisk part (applied to consignee per 4.2 normalization rules)
comparison_steps.description: Check if company names refer to the same juridical party.
comparison_steps.details: 'Xiamen Port Commerce Information Co., Ltd.' and 'Hongkong Topway Trading Co., Limited' are entirely different company names with no common words in the identity-bearing part. This is not a variation — these are different companies.

rules_applied.description: Prioritetsordning - primary billing fields checked first.
Juridisk part - company names are completely different entities.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: China/Hong Kong equivalence could make the countries match
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 4.6, China and Hong Kong are treated as equivalent for country verification. However, the company NAMES are entirely different ('Xiamen Port Commerce Information' vs 'Hongkong Topway Trading'). Country equivalence does not solve the fundamental name mismatch. The consignee company name from the certificate cannot be identified in the invoice at all.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Xiamen Port Commerce Information could be a subsidiary or agent of Hongkong Topway Trading
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 4.1.3.1 (applied to consignee): system may NOT assume group relations, subsidiary relationships, or agent/trading relationships. No evidence in the invoice connects these two entities.

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee: 'Xiamen Port Commerce Information Co., Ltd.' in China. Invoice has two party fields: SOLD TO and CONSIGNED TO, both showing 'Hongkong Topway Trading Co., Limited' in Hong Kong. The certificate consignee name 'Xiamen Port Commerce Information' does not appear anywhere in the invoice — not in any address block, header, footer, reference field, or any other text. The company names share no common identity-bearing words. Per rule 4.2.4, MISMATCH occurs when the consignee cannot be identified (company name missing from invoice). Even with China/HK country equivalence (rule 4.6), the company name mismatch is decisive. Result: MISMATCH.

checkpoint_results.invoice_value: Duosaica Testliner with sizing, SUBSTANCE: 75GSM, LOT 6: DUOSAICA TESTLINER WITH SIZING

comparison_steps.description: Check if article numbers are present in certificate.
comparison_steps.details: No article numbers are specified in the certificate.

comparison_steps.description: Identify the identity-bearing main designation and match against invoice.
comparison_steps.details: Identity-bearing main designation in certificate: 'Duosaica Testliner 75gsm'. Invoice DESCRIPTION: 'Duosaica Testliner with sizing'. Invoice LOT specification: 'LOT 6: DUOSAICA TESTLINER WITH SIZING, SUBSTANCE: 75GSM'. The core product name 'Duosaica Testliner' is identifiable in the invoice. The substance '75gsm'/'75GSM' is confirmed in the invoice lot specification. The addition 'with sizing' in the invoice is a non-identity-bearing addition per rule 4.3.4 — it does not change the product's model, variant, quality, or article identity. The certificate says '5x40' HC Containers' while invoice says '20X40' HC containers' — note the difference (5 vs 20), but this refers to container count for the shipment, not product identity. The product itself is the same: Duosaica Testliner 75gsm.

rules_applied.description: Controlled text matching - identity-bearing main designation 'Duosaica Testliner' with substance '75gsm' identified in both documents.
Non-identity-bearing additions - 'with sizing' in invoice does not change product identity.

normalizations_applied: Treatment of 'with sizing' as non-identity-bearing addition

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Container count difference (5 vs 20) could indicate a mismatch
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Container count is shipping/logistics information, not product identity. The certificate describes this specific lot (65 reels in 5 containers), while the invoice covers a larger shipment (234 reels in 20 containers). The product identity 'Duosaica Testliner 75gsm' is the same. Container count is not part of the identity-bearing main designation.

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate goods: 'Mill's standard, 5x40' HC Containers, 65 reels of Duosaica Testliner 75gsm'. Invoice goods: 'Duosaica Testliner with sizing, SUBSTANCE: 75GSM'. The identity-bearing main designation is 'Duosaica Testliner' with substance '75gsm'. 'Duosaica Testliner' appears explicitly in the invoice description and LOT specification. '75GSM' is confirmed via the SUBSTANCE field. 'with sizing' is a non-identity-bearing addition per 4.3.4. MATCH.

checkpoint_results.invoice_value: Total Weight Quantity: 491.163 MT, Total Unit Count: 234 Reels

comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate quantity 125.973 MT can be directly identified in the invoice.
comparison_steps.details: Invoice total weight is 491.163 MT, which does not match 125.973 MT. Invoice total unit count is 234 Reels, which does not match 65 reels. The value 125.973 does not appear anywhere in the invoice.

comparison_steps.description: Box 7 contains '125.973 mt'. This is the verification-grounding quantity.
comparison_steps.details: Box 7 value: 125.973 mt. This is a weight value in metric tons. No weight category (GW/NW) is specified.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states a single weight value (125.973 mt) without GW/NW specification.
comparison_steps.details: Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2: If certificate states ONE single weight value without weight category, and this value can be identified in the invoice, and the invoice does NOT contain a contradictory alternative weight value of another category → MATCH. However, the value 125.973 MT cannot be identified in the invoice at all. The invoice shows 491.163 MT total weight.

comparison_steps.description: Check if multiple invoices are present.
Check if unit conversion resolves the mismatch.
comparison_steps.details: Both values are already in MT. No conversion needed. The values themselves differ: 125.973 vs 491.163.

comparison_steps.description: Check if unit weight can be derived.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate gives total weight for 65 reels, not per-unit weight.

comparison_steps.step: 4.4.5.5 - Exception 3D: Sum of rows in same invoice
comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate quantity can be found as sum of rows.
comparison_steps.details: Invoice does not have multiple rows with individual weights that could sum to 125.973 MT.

comparison_steps.description: Check if row weights can be summed.
comparison_steps.details: Invoice does not contain per-row weight breakdowns.

comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate contains a shipment quantity in box 6.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate box 6 mentions '5x40' HC Containers' and '65 reels'. These could be interpreted as shipment packaging descriptions. However, '65 reels' is a specific quantity, not a generic shipment quantity like '1 package' or '1 shipment'. And '5x40' HC Containers' is also not a standard shipment quantity designation per rule 4.4.3.2.

comparison_steps.description: Assess whether the invoice lacks a comparable quantity entirely.
comparison_steps.details: The invoice DOES contain weight (491.163 MT) and unit count (234 Reels). However, neither matches the certificate's values (125.973 MT / 65 reels). This is because the invoice appears to cover a LARGER shipment (234 reels total across 20 containers) while the certificate covers only a PARTIAL shipment (65 reels in 5 containers). The invoice total is not comparable to the certificate's partial quantity. The value 125.973 MT is approximately 65/234 × 491.163 ≈ 136.3 MT, which does not match exactly either, so proportional calculation is not permitted. Since the invoice contains quantity data but the certificate's specific quantity cannot be identified due to the certificate covering only a portion of the invoice's shipment, this is a case where verification cannot be performed with certainty.

rules_applied.description: Main rule: direct identification attempted but value not found.

Invoice does contain weight data, but the certificate covers only a partial shipment and the specific value cannot be identified. This is not a complete absence of quantity data (which would trigger 4.4.6.1 directly). However, since the invoice quantity does not CONTRADICT the certificate (the certificate's quantity is a subset of the invoice total, not a conflicting value), and the value simply cannot be verified, MANUAL_REVIEW is appropriate.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: 125.973 MT could be verified by proportional calculation (65/234 of 491.163 MT)
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Proportional calculations are explicitly prohibited by rule 4.4.5. The result (≈136.3 MT) does not match anyway.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The quantity difference constitutes an explicit contradiction → MISMATCH
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 4.4.3.1, an explicit contradiction exists when BOTH documents state an explicit numeric value for the SAME quantity type and these values do not match. Here, the certificate states 125.973 MT for a partial shipment (65 reels in 5 containers), while the invoice states 491.163 MT for the total shipment (234 reels in 20 containers). These are not values for the SAME quantity — one is a partial shipment, the other is the full shipment total. The invoice does not state that the 5-container/65-reel portion weighs anything other than 125.973 MT. The values don't contradict each other — the certificate value simply cannot be verified because the invoice only provides the total. This situation is closer to 'the specific quantity cannot be identified in the invoice' rather than 'the invoice explicitly contradicts the certificate'.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MATCH because the certificate is a subset of the invoice
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The verification requires that the numeric value from the certificate can be IDENTIFIED in the invoice. 125.973 MT does not appear in the invoice. Subset logic is not permitted.

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate quantity (box 7): 125.973 mt. This value represents 65 reels in 5x40' HC containers. The invoice covers a larger shipment: 491.163 MT total for 234 reels in 20x40' HC containers. The value 125.973 does not appear anywhere in the invoice. The certificate appears to be for LOT 6 (partial shipment of the invoice total). Since the invoice does not break down weights per lot or per container group, the certificate's specific weight cannot be verified. However, the invoice does not explicitly contradict the certificate — it simply covers a larger quantity. No undantag 3A-3E applies. Per the spirit of 4.4.6.1 (adapted), where the verification cannot be completed because the invoice provides only aggregate data for a larger shipment, MANUAL_REVIEW is the appropriate outcome.

comparison_steps.description: Search for explicit origin field in invoice.
comparison_steps.details: Invoice contains explicit field 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: Spain'.

comparison_steps.description: Compare certificate origin with invoice origin after normalization.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate: 'Spain'. Invoice: 'Spain'. Exact match, no normalization needed.

rules_applied.description: Origin identified via explicit 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN' field in invoice.
MATCH: origin country explicitly identified in invoice matches certificate.

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate origin: 'Spain' (box 3). Invoice origin: 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: Spain'. Exact textual match. Additionally, PORT OF LOADING is 'BARCELONA, SPAIN', which is consistent (though not used for verification — origin is verified via the explicit COUNTRY OF ORIGIN field). Result: MATCH.

rule_evaluation_log.details: Invoice contains 'L/C No.: SM024IL005773900' and 'Irrevocable LC At Sight' in PAYMENT TERMS. However, the CERTIFICATE does not contain any LC reference. Rule 2.2 requires the LC reference to be in the certificate. LC exception not activated.
Document A is the certificate (Certificate of Origin, EU/ICC format). Document B is the invoice (Commercial Invoice from Th. Brunius & Co AB). Clear document roles, no ambiguity.
Consignor criterion met: 'Th.Brunius & Co AB' identifiable in invoice as issuer.
Consignor name found directly in invoice header and signature block.
Sweden confirmed via explicit address and VAT prefix SE.
Primary billing fields (SOLD TO, CONSIGNED TO) both show 'Hongkong Topway Trading Co., Limited' — different entity from certificate consignee 'Xiamen Port Commerce Information Co., Ltd.'
'Xiamen Port Commerce Information' not found anywhere in the invoice.
China and Hong Kong are equivalent for country verification. However, this only resolves the country comparison, not the company name mismatch.

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: Controlled text matching - identity-bearing main designation
rule_evaluation_log.details: 'Duosaica Testliner' with '75gsm' identified in both documents.
'with sizing' in invoice treated as non-identity-bearing addition.
125.973 MT not found in invoice. Invoice shows 491.163 MT total.
Both already in MT; values differ.
Certificate gives total, not per-unit weight.

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: Exception 3D - Sum of rows same invoice
rule_evaluation_log.details: Invoice does not have multiple rows with weights summing to 125.973 MT.
No per-row weight breakdown in invoice.
Certificate does not use a standard shipment quantity designation ('1 package', '1 shipment', etc.). '65 reels' and '5x40' HC Containers' are specific quantities, not generic shipment designations.
Explicit 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: Spain' field in invoice matches certificate's 'Spain'.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet anger mottagaren som Xiamen Port Commerce Information Co., Ltd. i Kina, men fakturan anger Hongkong Topway Trading Co., Limited i Hongkong som både köpare och mottagare. Xiamen Port Commerce Information förekommer inte någonstans i fakturan. Kvantiteten i certifikatet (125,973 MT för 65 rullar) kan inte verifieras mot fakturan som anger en större totalkvantitet (491,163 MT för 234 rullar).
overall_assessment.summary: Consignor (MATCH): Th.Brunius & Co AB verified in invoice. Consignee (MISMATCH): Certificate states Xiamen Port Commerce Information Co., Ltd. but invoice shows Hongkong Topway Trading Co., Limited — entirely different entities, certificate consignee not found anywhere in invoice. Goods description (MATCH): Duosaica Testliner 75gsm verified. Quantity (MANUAL_REVIEW): Certificate's 125.973 MT (partial shipment) cannot be identified in invoice which shows total 491.163 MT. Country of Origin (MATCH): Spain confirmed in both documents.
blocking_issues: Consignee mismatch: 'Xiamen Port Commerce Information Co., Ltd.' (certificate) vs 'Hongkong Topway Trading Co., Limited' (invoice) — different companies, certificate consignee not identifiable in invoice.
non_blocking_notes: Quantity verification inconclusive: certificate appears to cover LOT 6 (partial shipment of 65 reels/125.973 MT) while invoice covers full shipment (234 reels/491.163 MT). The specific certificate quantity cannot be verified against the invoice.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot faktura

sections.certificate_value: Th.Brunius & Co AB, PO Box 2516, SE-403 17, Gothenburg, Sweden
sections.invoice_value: Th. Brunius & Co AB, VAT NO: SE556011055201, PO Box 2516, SE-403 17 Gothenburg, Sweden
sections.rule_applied: Direkt identifiering av consignor i fakturans utställarblock. Företagsnamn och land (Sverige) överensstämmer efter normalisering av mellanslag.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets avsändare 'Th.Brunius & Co AB' identifieras i fakturans utställarblock som 'Th. Brunius & Co AB'. Skillnaden är enbart ett mellanslag efter 'Th.' — tillåten normalisering. Adress (PO Box 2516, SE-403 17 Gothenburg) och land (Sweden) överensstämmer exakt. VAT-prefix SE bekräftar Sverige.

sections.certificate_value: Xiamen Port Commerce Information Co., Ltd., 6/F, No. 31 North Donggang Road, Xiamen Area of China (Fujian) Pilot Free Trade Zone, China
sections.invoice_value: SOLD TO / CONSIGNED TO: Hongkong Topway Trading Co., Limited, Flat/Rm 2207 22/F West Tower Shun Tak Centre, No 168-200 Connaught Road, Central HK, Hong Kong
sections.rule_applied: Certifikatets mottagare kan inte identifieras i fakturan. Olika juridiska parter utan gemensamma namndelar.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger mottagaren som 'Xiamen Port Commerce Information Co., Ltd.' i Kina. Fakturans SOLD TO och CONSIGNED TO anger båda 'Hongkong Topway Trading Co., Limited' i Hongkong. Dessa är helt olika juridiska parter utan gemensamma identitetsbärande ord i företagsnamnet. 'Xiamen Port Commerce Information' förekommer inte någonstans i fakturan. Trots att Kina/Hongkong behandlas som ekvivalenta enligt regel 4.6 kvarstår att företagsnamnen är helt olika. Resultatet är MISMATCH.

sections.rule_applied: Kontrollerad textmatchning – identitetsbärande huvudbeteckning 'Duosaica Testliner 75gsm' identifierad i fakturan.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets identitetsbärande huvudbeteckning 'Duosaica Testliner' med substans '75gsm' kan identifieras i fakturan. Fakturan anger 'Duosaica Testliner with sizing' i beskrivningsfältet och 'SUBSTANCE: 75GSM' i lotspecifikationen. Tillägget 'with sizing' är icke-identitetsbärande per regel 4.3.4. MATCH.

sections.invoice_value: Total Weight Quantity: 491.163 MT, Total Unit Count: 234 Reels
sections.rule_applied: Certifikatets kvantitet (125,973 MT för 65 rullar) kan inte identifieras i fakturan som anger total 491,163 MT för 234 rullar. Certifikatet avser en delleverans som inte kan verifieras mot fakturans totalsiffror.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets box 7 anger 125.973 mt. Fakturan anger Total Weight Quantity: 491.163 MT och Total Unit Count: 234 Reels. Värdet 125.973 förekommer inte i fakturan. Certifikatet verkar avse LOT 6 (65 rullar i 5 containrar) av en större leverans (234 rullar i 20 containrar). Inga undantag 3A-3E är tillämpliga. Fakturan motsäger inte uttryckligen certifikatets kvantitet (den anger en total, inte en per-lot-uppdelning). Verifieringen kan inte genomföras med säkerhet — MANUAL_REVIEW.

sections.rule_applied: Ursprungsland identifierat i explicit COUNTRY OF ORIGIN-fält i fakturan.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger 'Spain' i ruta 3. Fakturan anger 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: Spain'. Exakt textmatchning utan normalisering. MATCH.

human_readable_report.overall_conclusion: NOT_IDENTICAL — Certifikatets mottagare (Xiamen Port Commerce Information Co., Ltd.) kan inte identifieras i fakturan som anger Hongkong Topway Trading Co., Limited. Ärendet kräver manuell handläggning.

output_quality_checks.consistency_details: Checkpoint 4.2 has MISMATCH → comparison_result is NOT_IDENTICAL. No contradictions between motivations and statuses. Checkpoint 4.4 has MANUAL_REVIEW with confidence 0.55 which is below 0.85 but this does not affect the overall result since comparison_result is already NOT_IDENTICAL due to consignee MISMATCH.
output_quality_checks.confidence_floor_check: Checkpoint 4.4 has confidence 0.55 for MANUAL_REVIEW status — confidence floor rule applies only to MATCH outcomes for IDENTICAL decisions. Since overall result is NOT_IDENTICAL, this is not blocking.
